annotations.label: Liouville number

anchor.snippet: a Liouville number is a real number

annotations.note: `Liouville x` is defined exactly as: for every `n`, there exist integers `a, b` with `1 < b` and `0 < |x - a/b| < 1/b^n`.

annotations.label: Liouville's constant

anchor.snippet: One of these examples is Liouville's constant

annotations.note: `liouvilleNumber m = ∑' i, 1/m^i!` generalizes the classical Liouville's constant (m = 2 is the named case discussed in the file docstring).

annotations.label: π and e are not Liouville numbers

anchor.snippet: It is known that π and e , although transcendental, are not Liouville numbers

annotations.note: No grep hit shows that `Real.pi` or `Real.exp 1` is `¬ Liouville`.

annotations.label: Existence of Liouville numbers

anchor.section: The existence of Liouville numbers (Liouville's constant)
anchor.snippet: Liouville numbers can be shown to exist by an explicit construction

annotations.note: Existence follows from `liouville_liouvilleNumber : 2 ≤ m → Liouville (liouvilleNumber m)`.

annotations.label: Construction of Liouville-type number

anchor.section: The existence of Liouville numbers (Liouville's constant)
anchor.snippet: For any integer

annotations.note: The parametric construction `liouvilleNumber m = ∑' i, 1/m^i!` matches the Wikipedia construction.

annotations.label: Liouville's constant (special case)

anchor.section: The existence of Liouville numbers (Liouville's constant)
anchor.snippet: the resulting number

annotations.note: The classical Liouville constant is `liouvilleNumber 2`; the docstring explicitly identifies this case.

annotations.label: Irrationality of the constructed number

anchor.section: The existence of Liouville numbers (Liouville's constant)
anchor.snippet: Since this base-

annotations.note: Irrationality of `liouvilleNumber m` is obtained by composing `liouville_liouvilleNumber` with `Liouville.irrational`, but there is no direct named lemma `Irrational (liouvilleNumber m)`.

annotations.label: Any such number is Liouville

anchor.section: The existence of Liouville numbers (Liouville's constant)
anchor.snippet: Therefore, any such

annotations.note: `liouville_liouvilleNumber` proves the constructed series is Liouville for every `m ≥ 2`.

annotations.label: Liouville numbers are irrational

anchor.snippet: Hence a Liouville number cannot be rational

annotations.note: `Liouville.irrational : Liouville x → Irrational x` is proved in Basic.lean.

annotations.label: No Liouville number is algebraic

anchor.snippet: No Liouville number is algebraic

annotations.note: `Liouville.transcendental : Liouville x → Transcendental ℤ x` directly states no Liouville number is algebraic.

annotations.label: Liouville's theorem on diophantine approximation

anchor.snippet: If

annotations.note: `exists_pos_real_of_irrational_root` gives the constant `A > 0` with `(b+1)^(deg f) · |α - a/(b+1)| · A ≥ 1` for every irrational algebraic `α`.

annotations.label: Liouville numbers are transcendental

anchor.snippet: Hence a Liouville number cannot be algebraic, and therefore must be transcendental

annotations.note: Same statement: `Liouville.transcendental` records exactly that every Liouville number is transcendental over ℤ.

annotations.label: Not every transcendental is Liouville

anchor.snippet: not every transcendental number is a Liouville number

annotations.note: No counterexample of a transcendental non-Liouville real is recorded in Mathlib (no `Transcendental ∧ ¬ Liouville` example found).

annotations.label: e is transcendental but not Liouville

anchor.snippet: Using the explicit continued fraction expansion of e

annotations.note: No theorem `Transcendental ℤ (Real.exp 1)` or `¬ Liouville (Real.exp 1)` is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: π is not Liouville (Mahler 1953)

anchor.snippet: Mahler proved in 1953 that π is another such example

annotations.note: Mahler's bound `¬ Liouville Real.pi` is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Liouville number from digits of π

anchor.snippet: where the digits are zero except in positions

annotations.note: No construction tied to digits of π producing a Liouville number is formalized.

annotations.label: Uncountability of Liouville numbers

anchor.snippet: Since the set of all sequences of non-null digits has the cardinality of the continuum

annotations.note: No cardinality statement about `{x | Liouville x}` was found (it can be deduced from density+`Gδ` but is not a named lemma).

annotations.label: Liouville numbers are dense

anchor.snippet: the Liouville numbers form a dense subset of the set of real numbers

annotations.note: `dense_liouville : Dense {x | Liouville x}` is proved in Residual.lean.

annotations.label: Lebesgue measure of Liouville numbers is zero

anchor.snippet: its Lebesgue measure

annotations.note: `volume_setOf_liouville : volume {x : ℝ | Liouville x} = 0` is proved in Measure.lean.

annotations.label: Measure of transcendentals is infinite

anchor.snippet: the Lebesgue measure of the set of all real transcendental numbers is infinite

annotations.note: No direct statement that `volume {x : ℝ | Transcendental ℤ x} = ∞` was found (only that the algebraic reals are countable, in `AlgebraicCard`).

annotations.label: Hausdorff dimension zero

anchor.snippet: the set of Liouville numbers has Hausdorff dimension 0

annotations.note: Grep for `Hausdorff` together with `Liouville` returns no Mathlib hits.

annotations.label: L is a dense G-delta set

anchor.section: Structure of the set of Liouville numbers
anchor.snippet: L is comeagre

annotations.note: `eventually_residual_liouville` + `IsGδ.setOf_liouville` + `dense_liouville` together state that the Liouville set is a dense `Gδ` (residual) subset of ℝ.

annotations.label: Liouville–Roth irrationality measure

anchor.snippet: The Liouville–Roth irrationality measure

annotations.note: `LiouvilleWith p x` captures the per-exponent Liouville condition underlying the irrationality measure, but the sup/μ(x) = inf{p : ¬ LiouvilleWith p x} itself is not defined.
